note: MCP servers consumed by the agent-pipe MCP client (probed on startup, surfaced as 'mcp.<id>.<tool>'). The Tier-0 servers below ship DISABLED (enabled:false) -- the base image bakes NO MCP server binaries, so they are INERT until an operator installs the binary (npx/uvx) and flips enabled:true in an /etc or ~/.config overlay. Nothing here connects out without explicit operator action. P4 2026-06-11: per-server tier/namespace/taint fields are OPT-IN metadata (old loaders ignore them); see usr/share/mios/doc/concepts/mcp-tools-optimization.md (P4).

servers.label: Playwright (headless browser)
examples: open a web page in a browser
click a button on the page
fill in and submit a form on a website
take a screenshot of the rendered page
extract the text from a page after it loads
navigate to a url and read the content
scrape a site that needs javascript to render
servers.note: Headless browser automation (23 tools: navigate/click/type/snapshot/...). BIGGEST capability gap. taint=untrusted_web -> browsing TAINTS the session (it loads attacker-controllable content) so the Semantic Firewall refuses high-privilege verbs afterward. ENABLE (verified live 2026-06-11): `npm i -g @playwright/mcp@0.0.76`, then `mkdir -p /var/lib/mios/ai/{tmp,.npm,.cache} && chown -R mios-ai:mios-ai /var/lib/mios/ai/{tmp,.npm,.cache}` (the agent-pipe spawns the stdio server as mios-ai, which de-escalates npm -> ALL writable paths MUST be mios-ai-owned: cwd/HOME/cache/TMPDIR or npm dies with EACCES), then flip enabled:true. For real page actions also `npx playwright install chromium` (tools/list works without it). PIN the version (no @latest -- 2026 MCP-SDK supply-chain RCE).

servers.label: DuckDB (analytical SQL)
servers.note: In-process analytical SQL over local files (CSV/Parquet/JSON) -- offline, no cloud. Use :memory: or a local .duckdb path. Operator: `uv tool install mcp-server-motherduck==0.6.1`, then enable.

servers.label: Postgres (restricted, reads the local pgvector)
servers.note: crystaldba/postgres-mcp (maintained fork) in --access-mode=restricted -> read-only/safe SQL against the EXISTING local pgvector (the agent plane's own DB). Loopback only. Operator: `uv tool install postgres-mcp==0.3.0`, confirm the mios-ai pg role + DATABASE_URI, then enable. Restricted mode is mandatory -- never run unrestricted against the agent DB.

x-mios.vendor_default: all-disabled (inert until operator installs the binary + enables)

per_server_fields.tier: core|common|rare -- rare keeps the server's tools OUT of the cached core prefix + the default tail; reachable on-demand via tool_search / semantic match.
per_server_fields.namespace: a model-facing prefix (browser_/duckdb_/pg_) for cheap group-masking + readability.
per_server_fields.taint: untrusted_web (or '') -- a non-empty taint marks the server's tools as a TAINT SOURCE: once called, the session is tainted and the Semantic Firewall refuses _HIGH_PRIVILEGE_VERBS until the operator clears the chain (lethal-trifecta-by-construction).

x-mios.security: PIN every version (no @latest). Destructive ops gate on the HITL/firewall, NEVER the server's self-asserted destructiveHint. Prefer the signed Docker MCP Catalog where available.
x-mios.tools_native_to_hermes: MiOS-Hermes (default sub-agent at :8642) ships its OWN tool catalog; MCP is the second surface, layered ON TOP for cross-agent / cross-host tool sharing.
